title: 1 Speciedaler - Christian VIII (Type 2)

obverse.description: Head of King Christian VIII facing right. Small crown as mintmark below head and engraver's initials on neck-cut.
obverse.lettering:
CHRISTIANVS VIII   |   D:G:DANIÆ V:G:REX
H. C.
♔
obverse.lettering_translation: Christian VIII by the Grace of God, King of Denmark, of the Wends and the Goths

reverse.description: Crowned grater shield with Arms of Denmark and its territories (adorned with the collar of the Order of the Elephant) is held at both sides by wildmen armed with clubs. A wide crowned mantle is behind the shield and supporters and also divides value in letters above all. Engraver name on bar below supporters feet with date below dividing mintmark at sides.

comments:
Two main differences allow to distinguish Km# 741 from Km# 720:
- Obverse legend is CHRISTIANUS in km# 720 and CHRISTIANVS in km# 741
- In km# 720 the engraver's name is on the bar below the shield on reverse, while in Km# 741 the initials are engraved on neck-cut in obverse portrait (even if often are poorly visible or even unreadable)
